changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to GDPR Article 32. However, the audit logging function name was too generic and needed to be more specific to the API server, which is the primary component responsible for audit logging in Kubernetes.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names specify the relevant Kubernetes component or resource for clarity.
title: Article 32 Security of processing
step1_reasoning: Article 32 of GDPR focuses on the security of processing, which includes ensuring data confidentiality, integrity, and availability. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to several security domains: encryption of secrets and etcd for data at rest, audit logging for tracking access and modifications, and network policies to restrict unauthorized access. These controls align with the security requirements of GDPR Article 32.
